content:
Because I am so methodical when it comes to building the entirety of my class on Blackboard, Canvas, or other LMS before the semester begins, I find that I am able to constantly hone the class from semester to semester via feedback from my students as well as my supervisors. I openly and actively seek criticism as I believe that is the only way to improve and strengthen the work I present to my class. Specifically during the past year of remote learning, I'd often ask my students what they would like more of or less of or what they'd like to see change in the future.


During my graduate career I spent sometime in a few different service positions within the English Department that I believe contributed to my interests of working within the college system. While I was able to teach at least one class per semester, I also was a writing consultant for one year, I ran the Advanced Media and Productions Lab as a research assistant (I go into more detail on my resume, but we wrote a grant that replaced all the old hardware within that classroom), served on the English Graduate Student Society as treasurer, created and developed a national literature magazine that we represented at AWP every year, and also taught a community workshop from writers within the community. I found that as long as communication was open it was fairly easy and a smooth process to collaborate amongst different parts of the college. Also, I found that my flexibility served the college well as I was able to fit into whatever role that may have been needed by the college at that time.
context: From document: Student Success Statement — chunk 9 (chunk 9/11)
